title: Find the Winner of an Array Game

testcases.input: arr = [2,1,3,5,4,6,7], k = 2 Output: 5 Explanation: Let's see the rounds of the game: Round | arr | winner | win_count 1 | [2,1,3,5,4,6,7] | 2 | 1 2 | [2,3,5,4,6,7,1] | 3 | 1 3 | [3,5,4,6,7,1,2] | 5 | 1 4 | [5,4,6,7,1,2,3] | 5 | 2 So we can see that 4 rounds will be played and 5 is the winner because it wins 2 consecutive games.
testcases.expectedOutput: 5 Explanation: Let's see the rounds of the game: Round | arr | winner | win_count 1 | [2,1,3,5,4,6,7] | 2 | 1 2 | [2,3,5,4,6,7,1] | 3 | 1 3 | [3,5,4,6,7,1,2] | 5 | 1 4 | [5,4,6,7,1,2,3] | 5 | 2 So we can see that 4 rounds will be played and 5 is the winner because it wins 2 consecutive games.

testcases.input: arr = [3,2,1], k = 10 Output: 3 Explanation: 3 will win the first 10 rounds consecutively.
testcases.expectedOutput: 3 Explanation: 3 will win the first 10 rounds consecutively.
